title: potentilla nivea subsp. kuznetzowii
intro: Potentilla nivea subsp. kuznetzowii, also known as the white potentilla, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Potentilla nivea subsp. kuznetzowii often include infusions and decoctions of the plant's parts, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat various ailments.
